name: Freddie Prinze
blurb: A December 1973 appearance on The Tonight Show led to his starring role as Chico in the NBC sitcom Chico and the Man from 1974 until his death.
bio: Freddie Prinze was an American stand-up comedian and actor who rose rapidly in the early 1970s, billing himself as a Hungarian-Puerto Rican Hunga-rican in New York comedy clubs. A December 1973 appearance on The Tonight Show led to his starring role as Chico in the NBC sitcom Chico and the Man from 1974 until his death. He died by a self-inflicted gunshot in 1977 at age 22; a later civil jury found the death medication-induced and accidental.

timeline.ev: Breakthrough debut on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Begins starring as Chico on NBCs Chico and the Man
Releases the live comedy album Looking Good
Stars in the TV movie The Million Dollar Rip-Off
Receives a posthumous star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame

accolades: First young comedian invited to sit with Carson on a debut appearance (1973)
Star of the hit NBC sitcom Chico and the Man
Posthumous star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame (2004)
